rationale: The active war remains the dominant reality; confirmed US NATO asset reductions are a negative background signal for Western security commitments, while the Iran deal is largely irrelevant to Ukraine's immediate situation.

top_events.title: Active war with Russia — ongoing
top_events.summary: No major new escalation this cycle, but the war continues at the same high operational tempo with persistent air strike and infrastructure threats across Ukraine.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets deployed for NATO operations in Europe
top_events.summary: Reduced US military presence in Europe weakens the broader Western deterrence posture, an indirect but real negative signal for Ukraine's security environment and Western resolve.

top_events.title: Iran says deal with US has 'never been closer'
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal could free up US diplomatic bandwidth and ease global energy revenues for Russia indirectly, but the direct impact on Ukraine's war situation is minimal.
